title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward has impressed with 12 stolen bases in 12 spring training games, leading the Red Sox in steals.
Max Ferguson, showing positional versatility, hit a grand slam and leads the Red Sox with 7 RBIs this spring.
Tayron Guerrero has pitched six relief innings with six strikeouts and one earned run, showing promise in Boston's bullpen.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Bruins in 5-on-5 goals with 13 this season.
Bruins considered trading Arvidsson for draft picks but retained him for potential playoff contributions.
Coach Marco Sturm supports Arvidsson's continued role, highlighting his production and synergy with teammates.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots traded center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears, necessitating a search for a new left guard.
Jared Wilson is expected to move to center, leaving Isaac Seumalo as a top free-agent guard option.
David Edwards, Joel Bitonio, Elgton Jenkins, and Emmanuel Pregnon are other potential linemen for the Patriots.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry from Stockton, CA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry has been involved with Make-A-Wish for 15 years, meeting many children affected by serious illnesses.
His marathon effort aims to honor both current Wish children and those who have passed away.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots poised to sign Seahawks' edge rusher Boye Mafe with a projected three-year, $48 million contract.
ESPN's Dan Graziano predicts Colts WR Alec Pierce will sign with the Chargers despite Patriots' interest.
New England has $61.6 million in cap space, allowing potential deals for Mafe and other free agents.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues with his 'Under the Powerlines Tour', featuring politically charged songs.
Welles played a 20-song set, including covers of Black Sabbath, Radiohead, and John Denver songs.
His performance included songs about current events, blending sarcastic lyrics with folk and alt-rock influences.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a 387-foot home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea.
Yoshida is 4-for-7 at the plate in Japan's first two World Baseball Classic games, contributing four RBIs.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game at the Tokyo Dome while visiting Japan with his wife.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026 to support Mass. Eye and Ear.
Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, grew up in Roslindale and is a teacher in Boston Public Schools.
She has been a patient at Mass. Eye and Ear for nearly a decade due to two eye conditions.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Legler has been with ESPN since 2000 and is recognized as its longest-tenured NBA analyst.
Legler expresses enthusiasm for his new role and praises the Boston Celtics' performance this season.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists in his TD Garden NBA debut with the Mavericks.
Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics after 298 days, scoring 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists.
Flagg’s NBA visit to Boston attracted many Maine fans, highlighting his local connection and family support.
